title: ranunculus suprasericeus
intro:
Ranunculus suprasericeus, also known as the Rice Puff Caltha or Rice-flowered Caltha, is a plant species belonging to the genus Ranunculus, characterized by its white and yellow flowers resembling rice puffs.

The Ranunculus suprasericeus plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its rhizome, which contains a compound called ranunculin that has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions.

Research on the plant has revealed several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat.

The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the dried rhizome in hot water, and it is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat respiratory issues and digestive problems.
